chunks.text: 1 ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI Constitution Petition No.613 of 2024 Date Orders with Signatures of Judges For hearing 1. For hearing of CMA No.5112/2024 2. For hearing of main case 13.05.2025. Ms. Darakhshan Jahan, Advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Muhammad Javed, AAG Through this petition, petitioner seeks direction to the concerned SHO to produce her father allegedly detained by the Respondents No.1 to 3, who are his step brothers and sister. Learned counsel submits that the Respondents who are step brothers and sister, have detained Muhammad Khan Baloch, alleged detenue to usurp his properties as being an old person petitioner’s father has been suffering from multiple diseases including dementia. She further submits that the Respondents No.1 to 3 are not allowing the petitioner to meet with her father. Heard learned counsel and perused the material available on record. To a query learned counsel submits that father of the detenue is aged about 78 years. In the memo of petition, petitioner has not spelt out since when her father has been in wrongful custody of his step brothers and sister, who are attempting to usurp her father’s properties. Moreover, perusal of the file reveals that petitioner presented instant petition in office on 08.05.2024 for recovery of her father from the illegal custody of the Respondents No.1, 2 and 3 and thereafter on 15.6.2024 addressed a complaint to the SSP Malir for taking action against her uncles Nasir Ali and Amir Ali (Respondents No.1 and 2 herein) who not only trying to usurp properties of her father but also not allowing her to meet and provide medical treatment to him.

In the memo of petition, petitioner has not spelt out since when her father has been in wrongful custody of his step brothers and sister, who are attempting to usurp her father’s properties. Moreover, perusal of the file reveals that petitioner presented instant petition in office on 08.05.2024 for recovery of her father from the illegal custody of the Respondents No.1, 2 and 3 and thereafter on 15.6.2024 addressed a complaint to the SSP Malir for taking action against her uncles Nasir Ali and Amir Ali (Respondents No.1 and 2 herein) who not only trying to usurp properties of her father but also not allowing her to meet and provide medical treatment to him. I do not see any merit in the instant petition which is accordingly dismissed leaving the petitioner at liberty to pursue legal remedies permissible under the law, if so advised. Judge
